task_goal: Improve repeatable handling of bulk upload accepts duplicate external ids in the same batch: A bulk upload path accepts two rows with the same external ID in one batch and processes them as if they were unique.
winner_pattern: Likely missing in-memory uniqueness validation before row processing begins
loser_pattern: A broad fix could accidentally reject legitimate repeated references across different import modes

source_bug.title: Bulk upload accepts duplicate external IDs in the same batch

preconditions: Local import worker or import validation path is available
A fixture CSV or JSON batch with two rows sharing one external ID exists
steps: Run the bulk upload flow with a batch containing duplicate external IDs
Inspect the validation response and the resulting imported records
reproduction.expected_behavior: The batch is rejected up front with a duplicate-ID validation message and no records are created or updated.
reproduction.actual_behavior: The batch passes validation and processing continues with conflicting row identity.

manual_checks: Run the duplicate-ID fixture again and confirm the batch is rejected before processing
Confirm a valid batch with unique IDs still imports successfully

rollback.rollback_rule: Rollback on any hard-check failure or if valid uploads begin failing on unique IDs
rollback.notes: Keep the last known passing batch-validation behavior and a valid fixture result for comparison.

source_bug.scope_notes: Keep the change inside batch validation and direct import processing entrypoints. Do not redesign persistence or deduplication strategy beyond what is required to reject duplicates in one batch.
